stemHtml: On the 15th October, a pilot is departing from Auckland, New Zealand (37°00’S 174°47’E position west of the international Date Line) to Honolulu in Hawaii, US (21°19’N 157°55’W position east of the international Date line) where LT = UTC - 10 hours. When departing the aircrafts master clock displays 08:15 UTC. After landing, the pilot records a flight time of 8 hours and 37 minutes. Which of the below statements is correct?

options.a: Upon arrival the master clock of the aircraft displays 16:52 UTC and the local dates in the 16<sup>th</sup> October.
options.b: The aircraft arrives in Honolulu at 16:52 LT on the 15<sup>th</sup> October.
options.c: The aircraft arrives in Honolulu at 06:52 LT on the 15<sup>th</sup> October.
options.d: When the aircraft arrives in Honolulu, the local date in Auckland is still the 15<sup>th</sup> October.
